![Marmidon company logo](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

**MARMIDON ENTERPRISE SOFTWARE · WEBSITE PROGRAMME**

**MARM-WEB-WDD-002**

**Website Design Document**

Brand System, UI Components & Page Blueprints

_The visual and interaction specification for <www.marmidon.com>. Defines the brand system, design tokens, reusable components and a wireframe blueprint for every page template._

| **Document**     | Website Design Document (WDD)           |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| **Programme**    | Marmidon Corporate Website Revamp 2026  |
| **Parent**       | MARM-WEB-PRD-001 (Requirements)         |
| **Version**      | 1.0 - Issued for Development            |
| **Brand colour** | #3588E4 (Marmidon Blue)                 |
| **Standards**    | WCAG 2.2 AA · Responsive / mobile-first |
| **Audience**     | Web designer & developer                |

**PREPARED BY**

**Marmidon Website Programme - Business Analysis Workstream**

Senior Business Analyst (Corporate & Enterprise Web)

_CONFIDENTIAL - For the Marmidon website development team and authorised stakeholders only._

**Table of Contents**

# **1\. Purpose & How to Use This Document**

_This document turns the requirements into a buildable visual system - tokens, components and page blueprints - so the site looks and behaves consistently at scale._

This Website Design Document (WDD) is the design authority for the Marmidon website. It elaborates the look, feel and interaction behind the requirements in **MARM-WEB-PRD-001**. Page structure references the information architecture in **MARM-WEB-SIA-003**, and content slots reference **MARM-WEB-CPM-005**.

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Build with tokens and components, not one-off pages**

Define the design tokens (§3) and the component library (§6) first, then assemble pages (§7) from those components. This guarantees visual consistency, faster build, and a site Marmidon can extend without it drifting.

### **Design principles**

#### **Clarity over cleverness**

Explain a complex product simply. Generous whitespace, strong hierarchy, no jargon-as-decoration.

#### **Trust by design**

Proof, certifications and real numbers are first-class visual elements, not afterthoughts.

#### **Conversion-led**

Every template guides the eye to a clear next step; the demo CTA is always within reach.

#### **Accessible & fast**

Contrast, focus states, semantic structure and performance are design constraints, not options.

#### **Consistent & scalable**

One system of tokens and components renders 11 module pages and 12 sector pages without bespoke work.

**SECTION 2**

**Brand Identity**

Logo, colour and the Marmidon look

# **2\. Brand Identity**

## **2.1 The Marmidon logo**

The Marmidon mark is a cloud with a pixel-dissolve edge enclosing the wordmark - signalling cloud-delivered, digital enterprise software. It is the primary brand asset and appears in the header and footer of every page.

![Marmidon company logo](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

### **Logo usage rules**

| **Do**                                                        | **Don't**                                     |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| Use the supplied logo at full quality (SVG preferred for web) | Stretch, distort, rotate or recolour the mark |
| Maintain clear space ≥ the height of the cloud on all sides   | Crowd the logo against text, edges or imagery |
| Place on white or very light backgrounds                      | Place on busy photos or low-contrast colours  |
| Provide a reversed/white version for dark footers             | Add shadows, outlines or effects              |
| Keep minimum width ≥ 110px on screen for legibility           | Let it shrink until ".COM" becomes unreadable |

**MUST-HAVE · Provide the logo as SVG**

For crispness at every size and best performance, the developer must use a vector (SVG) version of the logo in the header/footer. A high-resolution PNG with transparency is the acceptable fallback. The supplied JPG is a reference only.

## **2.2 Colour system**

The palette is built from the Marmidon blue (#3588E4). Blues carry brand and interaction; navy anchors headings and dark sections; accents add meaning (success, guidance, alerts); neutrals carry text and surfaces.

#### **Primary & brand**

| **Marmidon Blue**<br><br>#3588E4 | **Interactive Blue**<br><br>#1E66C4 | **Deep Navy**<br><br>#0F2D5C | **Steel**<br><br>#1F4E79 |
| -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------ |

#### **Accent (meaning)**

| **Teal**<br><br>#0E8F84 | **Success**<br><br>#1F8A4C | **Guidance**<br><br>#C9851A | **Alert**<br><br>#C2412B | **Highlight**<br><br>#6A3FA0 |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------- |

#### **Neutrals & surfaces**

| **Ink (text)**<br><br>#1F2933 | **Muted**<br><br>#5A6470 | **Hairline**<br><br>#D5DBE2 | **Surface Sky**<br><br>#E8F2FC | **White**<br><br>#FFFFFF |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------ |

### **Colour application & contrast**

| **Token**            | **Hex** | **Primary use**                             | **Min. contrast**                |
| -------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| **Marmidon Blue**    | #3588E4 | Primary buttons, key accents, active states | Use navy text on it where needed |
| **Interactive Blue** | #1E66C4 | Text links, hover, focus rings              | AA on white                      |
| **Deep Navy**        | #0F2D5C | H1, dark bands, footer                      | AA+ on white                     |
| **Ink**              | #1F2933 | Body text                                   | AA+ on white/sky                 |
| **Success**          | #1F8A4C | Confirmation, positive proof                | AA on white                      |
| **Alert**            | #C2412B | Errors, must-have flags                     | AA on white                      |

**BEST PRACTICE · Contrast is a design rule, not a QA fix**

All text must meet WCAG 2.2 AA contrast (≥ 4.5:1 for body, ≥ 3:1 for large text and UI components). Never place light-blue text on white, or body text on the Marmidon blue without checking contrast. Buttons and focus states must remain distinguishable for colour-blind users (don't rely on colour alone).

**SECTION 3**

**Typography & Design Tokens**

The type scale, spacing and radius system

# **3\. Typography & Design Tokens**

## **3.1 Typeface**

A clean, highly legible sans-serif is used throughout, consistent with the corporate brand and optimised for screen reading. Inter, Source Sans 3 or a system-UI stack are all suitable web choices; the brand's document font is Calibri. The developer should select one performant web font family with weights 400/500/600/700 and define it as a token.

## **3.2 Type scale (responsive)**

Desktop sizes shown; scale down ~10-20% on mobile via fluid type. Maintain the hierarchy ratios.

| **Token** | **Element**    | **Size (desktop)** | **Weight** | **Line-height** | **Colour**           |
| --------- | -------------- | ------------------ | ---------- | --------------- | -------------------- |
| display   | Hero headline  | 48-60px            | 700        | 1.1             | Navy / White on dark |
| h1        | Page title     | 36-40px            | 700        | 1.15            | Navy                 |
| h2        | Section title  | 28-32px            | 700        | 1.2             | Interactive Blue     |
| h3        | Sub-section    | 22-24px            | 600        | 1.25            | Steel                |
| h4        | Minor heading  | 18-20px            | 600        | 1.3             | Teal                 |
| body-lg   | Lead paragraph | 18-20px            | 400        | 1.6             | Ink/Muted            |
| body      | Body text      | 16px               | 400        | 1.6             | Ink                  |
| small     | Captions/meta  | 13-14px            | 500        | 1.4             | Muted                |
| button    | Button label   | 15-16px            | 600        | 1               | Contextual           |

## **3.3 Spacing, radius, elevation tokens**

| **Token group**     | **Values**                                         | **Usage**                                           |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| **Spacing scale**   | 4 · 8 · 12 · 16 · 24 · 32 · 48 · 64 · 96px         | All margins, padding, gaps use the scale (8px base) |
| **Container width** | Max 1200-1280px, centred, 16-24px side gutters     | Page content width                                  |
| **Grid**            | 12-column, 24px gutter (desktop); stacks on mobile | Layout                                              |
| **Radius**          | sm 6px · md 10px · lg 16px · pill 999px            | Cards, buttons, inputs                              |
| **Elevation**       | sm/md/lg soft shadows; navy at low opacity         | Cards, menus, sticky header                         |
| **Breakpoints**     | sm 480 · md 768 · lg 1024 · xl 1280px              | Responsive behaviour                                |

**SECTION 4**

**Imagery, Iconography & Motion**

# **4\. Imagery, Iconography & Motion**

## **4.1 Photography & illustration**

- Professional, authentic business imagery - real operations, people and workplaces over generic stock where possible.
- Product visuals: clean screenshots or annotated UI mock-ups of Marmidon modules, framed in device or browser chrome.
- Optional light abstract motifs echoing the logo's pixel-cloud for hero and section backgrounds - subtle, never noisy.
- Every image needs descriptive alt text (enforced in the CMS); decorative images marked as such.

## **4.2 Iconography**

Use one consistent icon set (line or duotone) across modules, features and benefits. Each of the 11 modules gets a distinct, recognisable icon reused everywhere that module appears (menu, cards, pages) for instant recognition.

## **4.3 Motion & interaction**

| **Interaction**             | **Behaviour**                        | **Constraint**                 |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ |
| Hover (buttons/cards/links) | Subtle lift, colour shift, underline | ≤ 200ms, ease-out              |
| Scroll reveal               | Gentle fade/slide-in for sections    | Respect prefers-reduced-motion |
| Mega-menu                   | Fade/slide open; keyboard accessible | No motion-only meaning         |
| Sticky header               | Condenses on scroll                  | No layout shift (CLS)          |
| Form feedback               | Inline validation, success state     | Accessible, announced to AT    |

**BEST PRACTICE · Motion must never block, distract or exclude**

Animation is light and purposeful. Honour the user's reduced-motion preference, never gate content behind animation, and never let motion cause layout shift that harms Core Web Vitals.

**SECTION 5**

**Layout & Responsive Foundations**

# **5\. Layout & Responsive Foundations**

## **5.1 Grid & containers**

A 12-column grid on a centred container (max ~1280px) governs desktop layout, collapsing to a single column on mobile. Components define their own column spans; sections breathe with the spacing scale.

## **5.2 Responsive behaviour by breakpoint**

| **Breakpoint**        | **Layout behaviour**                                                                                               |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Mobile < 768px**    | Single column; hamburger nav with full module list; CTAs full-width; cards stack; sticky bottom or header demo CTA |
| **Tablet 768-1024px** | 2-column card grids; condensed nav; mega-menu adapts to tap                                                        |
| **Desktop ≥ 1024px**  | Full grid; hover mega-menu; multi-column footers; max container width applies                                      |

**MUST-HAVE · Mobile-first is mandatory**

Design and build for the smallest screen first, then enhance. Decision-makers research vendors on mobile; a desktop-only experience loses pipeline. Every template in §7 must be verified on mobile.

**SECTION 6**

**Component Library**

Reusable building blocks for every page

# **6\. Component Library**

Build these reusable components once; assemble pages from them. Each maps to requirements in the PRD.

## **6.1 Buttons & links**

| **Variant**         | **Appearance**                                    | **Use**                                 |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| **Primary**         | Filled Marmidon Blue, white label, md radius      | "Request a Demo" - one per view ideally |
| **Secondary**       | Outlined navy/blue, transparent fill              | "Contact Sales", "Learn more"           |
| **Tertiary / text** | Text + arrow, underline on hover                  | In-content links, "See all"             |
| **States**          | Default · hover · focus(ring) · active · disabled | All required; focus ring always visible |

## **6.2 Core components (specifications)**

| **Component**               | **Specification**                                                                                                               | **PRD ref.**               |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- |
| **Header / nav bar**        | Logo (left), primary nav with Products mega-menu, search, secondary CTA, primary demo CTA (right). Sticky, condenses on scroll. | FR-NAV-01..05, 09          |
| **Mega-menu**               | Panel exposing all 11 modules grouped, plus Solutions/Industries column and a featured promo slot.                              | FR-NAV-02                  |
| **Footer**                  | Mega-footer: Modules · Sectors · Resources · Company · Legal columns, social, contact, reversed logo, copyright.                | FR-NAV-06                  |
| **Hero**                    | Headline + sub-headline + primary/secondary CTA + supporting visual; variant for inner pages (compact).                         | FR-HOME-01, FR-MOD-02      |
| **Module card**             | Icon, module name, one-line benefit, link. Used in suite grids and menus.                                                       | FR-HOME-02                 |
| **Sector card**             | Icon/image, sector name, hook, link.                                                                                            | FR-HOME-03                 |
| **Feature/capability list** | Icon + title + short copy, in 2-3 column grid.                                                                                  | FR-MOD-03                  |
| **Benefit/outcome block**   | Outcome statement with supporting stat or icon.                                                                                 | FR-MOD-04                  |
| **Proof band**              | Client logo wall and/or key metrics strip.                                                                                      | FR-HOME-04, FR-PROOF-03/05 |
| **Testimonial**             | Quote, name, role, company, optional photo/logo. Reusable.                                                                      | FR-PROOF-04                |
| **Case-study card**         | Sector tag, client, headline result, link.                                                                                      | FR-PROOF-01                |
| **CTA band**                | Full-width navy/blue band: headline + demo + contact.                                                                           | FR-HOME-08, FR-CONV        |
| **Form**                    | Labelled fields, inline validation, accessible errors, success state, spam protection.                                          | FR-CONV-01..07             |
| **FAQ accordion**           | Expandable Q&A, keyboard accessible.                                                                                            | FR-MOD-08, FR-PRICE-04     |
| **Breadcrumbs**             | Trail reflecting IA, below hero on inner pages.                                                                                 | FR-NAV-07                  |
| **Pricing/edition card**    | Edition name, what's included, CTA. Comparison-friendly.                                                                        | FR-PRICE-02                |
| **Blog/resource card**      | Image, category tag, title, excerpt, date, link.                                                                                | FR-RES-01/02               |
| **Cookie consent**          | Compliant banner; manage-preferences modal.                                                                                     | FR-NAV-10, FR-X-05         |

**SECTION 7**

**Page Template Blueprints**

A wireframe for every template, top to bottom

# **7\. Page Template Blueprints**

Each blueprint lists page zones top-to-bottom with the components and content each contains. Read the coloured left-bar blocks as stacked sections of the page. These map 1:1 to the site map (SIA-003) and content matrix (CPM-005).

## **7.1 Homepage**

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Homepage (template: home)**

**HEADER Global header + nav + demo CTA**

Logo, nav, mega-menu, search, Contact Sales, Request a Demo

**HERO Value proposition**

Headline (what Marmidon is), sub-headline, primary + secondary CTA, product/brand visual

**PROOF BAND Instant credibility**

Client logos and/or key numbers (years, clients, modules, sectors)

**MODULE SUITE All 11 modules**

Grid of module cards, each linking to its page

**WHY MARMIDON Differentiators**

3-5 benefit/outcome blocks (integration, breadth, local support)

**SECTOR STRIP All 12 sectors**

Sector cards linking to sector pages

**FEATURED PROOF Story**

Featured case study + testimonial component

**RESOURCES TEASER Nurture**

Up to 3 latest insight cards

**CTA BAND Convert**

Demo + contact band

**FOOTER Global footer**

Mega-footer + legal + social

## **7.2 Module page (template applies to all 11)**

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Module Page (template: module)**

**HEADER Global header**

As global

**BREADCRUMB Context**

Home › Products › \[Module\]

**HERO (compact) Module value**

Module name, one-line outcome, module-tagged demo CTA, visual

**CAPABILITIES What it does**

Feature/capability grid (≥ 6), icon + title + copy

**OUTCOMES Why it matters**

Benefit/outcome blocks framing features as results

**SCREENSHOT Show the product**

Annotated module screenshot / placeholder slot

**WORKS WITH Integration story**

Cross-links to related modules

**PROOF Trust**

Relevant testimonial / case-study card

**FAQ Objections**

Module FAQ accordion

**CTA BAND Convert**

Module-tagged demo + contact

**FOOTER Global footer**

As global

## **7.3 Sector / Industry page (template applies to all 12)**

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Sector Page (template: sector)**

**HEADER Global header**

As global

**BREADCRUMB Context**

Home › Solutions › \[Sector\]

**HERO (compact) Sector value**

Sector name, pain-point hook, sector-tagged demo CTA

**PAIN → OUTCOME Re-frame**

Sector-specific challenges mapped to Marmidon outcomes

**RECOMMENDED MODULES Fit**

Cards for modules most relevant to this sector

**PROOF Sector trust**

Sector case study / logo (graceful fallback if none)

**CTA BAND Convert**

Sector-tagged demo + contact

**FOOTER Global footer**

As global

## **7.4 Customers index & case-study detail**

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Customers Index (template: customers)**

**HERO (compact) Proof promise**

"Trusted across \[n\] sectors" + filters

**FILTER BAR Find proof**

Filter by sector / module

**CASE GRID Browse**

Case-study cards

**LOGO WALL Breadth**

Client logos

**CTA BAND Convert**

Demo + contact

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Case Study Detail (template: case)**

**HERO Headline result**

Client, sector tag, headline metric

**CHALLENGE Context**

The client's problem

**SOLUTION What Marmidon did**

Modules deployed, approach

**RESULTS Quantified outcome**

Metrics + testimonial quote

**RELATED Keep exploring**

Related cases / modules

**CTA BAND Convert**

Demo + contact

## **7.5 Resources hub, article, pricing, partners, about, contact**

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Resources Hub (template: resources)**

**HERO (compact) Knowledge promise**

Intro + search

**FILTER/TYPE Navigate**

Filter by type (blog, guide, FAQ) & topic

**FEATURED Highlight**

Featured resource

**GRID Browse**

Resource/blog cards

**NEWSLETTER Nurture**

Subscribe component

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Article / Blog Post (template: article)**

**HERO Title**

Category tag, title, author, date

**BODY Content**

Rich text, images, quotes, headings

**INLINE CTA Convert**

Demo or gated-guide CTA

**RELATED Keep reading**

Related articles

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Pricing (template: pricing)**

**HERO (compact) How pricing works**

Plain-language pricing model

**EDITIONS/MODEL Compare**

Edition cards or module-based explanation

**WHAT DRIVES COST Transparency**

Factors: users, modules, deployment, support

**FAQ Commercial Qs**

Pricing FAQ accordion

**CTA BAND Convert**

Request a quote / demo

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Partners (template: partners)**

**HERO (compact) Programme**

Partner value proposition

**BENEFITS Why partner**

Benefit blocks

**HOW IT WORKS Process**

Steps to become a partner

**FORM Capture**

Become-a-partner enquiry form

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - About (template: about)**

**HERO (compact) Who we are**

Mission statement

**STORY Credibility**

What Marmidon does, the suite, the approach

**LEADERSHIP People**

Team profiles

**TRUST Proof**

Certifications, numbers, partners

**CTA BAND Convert**

Demo + contact / careers link

**◫ WIREFRAME BLUEPRINT - Contact (template: contact)**

**HERO (compact) Reach us**

"Talk to Marmidon"

**FORM Capture**

Contact form, validated & routed

**CHANNELS Alternatives**

Phone, email, address, map, hours

## **7.6 Worked wireframe annotations**

The blueprints in §7.1-§7.5 show **what** sits on each page. The annotated wireframes below show **how each element is built** - dimensions, spacing, responsive behaviour, and the content, interaction and accessibility rules for every numbered block. These are worked examples for the three highest-value templates; the same annotation method and tokens apply to the remaining templates.

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · How to read an annotated wireframe**

Each numbered block in the stacked wireframe corresponds to a numbered row in the annotation key beneath it.

"Design & spacing spec" uses the tokens from §3 (type scale, spacing, radius, grid, breakpoints). "Responsive behaviour" describes how the block reflows across the §5.2 breakpoints. The final column carries the content source (CPM-005), interaction and analytics hooks (TSR-004), and accessibility rules (§8).

**SPECIFICATION · Annotation conventions & shared tokens**

**Breakpoints:** sm (mobile) · md (tablet) · lg (desktop), per §5.2. "Stack" means multi-column collapses to a single column.

**Spacing:** space-1…space-8 scale from §3.3. Touch targets ≥ 44×44px. Container max-width and 12-column grid per §5.1.

**States:** every interactive element has default / hover / focus-visible / active / disabled states (§6.1). Focus indicators are mandatory (WCAG 2.2).

**◫ ANNOTATED WIREFRAME - Homepage (template: home)**

Read the numbered blocks top-to-bottom as the stacked page. Each number is annotated in the table beneath with its design spec, responsive behaviour, and content, interaction and accessibility notes.

| **1** | **HEADER Global header, navigation & demo CTA** |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------- |

| **2** | **HERO Value proposition + primary CTAs** |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------- |

| **3** | **PROOF BAND Client logos / key numbers** |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------- |

| **4** | **MODULE SUITE Grid of all 11 module cards** |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------- |

| **5** | **WHY MARMIDON 3-5 differentiator blocks** |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------ |

| **6** | **SECTOR STRIP Grid of all 12 sector cards** |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------- |

| **7** | **FEATURED PROOF Case metric + testimonial** |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------- |

| **8** | **RESOURCES Latest 3 insight cards** |
| ----- | ------------------------------------ |

| **9** | **CTA BAND Final demo + contact conversion** |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------- |

| **10** | **FOOTER Mega-footer, legal & social** |
| ------ | -------------------------------------- |

**Annotation key**

| **#**  | **Element**        | **Design & spacing spec**                                                                                                                                       | **Responsive behaviour**                                                                      | **Content · interaction · accessibility**                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------ | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **1**  | **Header / nav**   | Sticky top bar; height ~72px (lg) / 56px (sm); container max-width + gutters (§5.1); logo (SVG ~140px) left, nav centre, buttons right; padding space-4.        | Nav collapses to a hamburger below md; mega-menu becomes an accordion drawer.                 | Nav items from CMS navigation entries (CPM §3). "Request a Demo" = primary button (§6.1), fires cta_click. &lt;header&gt;/&lt;nav&gt; landmarks; skip-to-content link; focus-visible; mega-menu keyboard-operable. |
| **2**  | **Hero**           | Full-width band, min-height ~520px (lg); H1 in display size (§3.2); sub-headline; two buttons; brand visual right (≤200KB, TSR §4.3); vertical padding space-8. | Two-column → single column; text stacks above visual below md; H1 uses fluid type down-scale. | Copy from CPM §4 hero block. Primary "Request a Demo" (demo path), secondary "Explore modules". Exactly one H1; image alt text; text-over-image meets AA (add overlay if needed).                                  |
| **3**  | **Proof band**     | Horizontal strip; padding space-6; logos greyscale at ~40px height; even spacing.                                                                               | Wraps to 2-3 items per row on sm; optional slow carousel.                                     | Logos / metrics from CPM proof-strip block. Each logo &lt;img&gt; with alt = client name; decorative separators have empty alt.                                                                                    |
| **4**  | **Module suite**   | 12-col grid, cards 3-4 per row (lg); gap space-4; card component (§6.2) with radius + elevation tokens; equal height.                                           | 4 → 2 → 1 column across lg/md/sm.                                                             | 11 module cards from CMS, ordered per CPM §5 index (icon + name + one-liner + link). Whole card is one link; hover raises elevation; icon decorative; link text = module name (not "read more").                   |
| **5**  | **Why Marmidon**   | 3-5 blocks in a row; icon + H3 + short copy; equal-height; padding space-6.                                                                                     | Row → stacked single column below md.                                                         | Differentiators from CPM §4. Each block led by an H3; icons decorative (aria-hidden).                                                                                                                              |
| **6**  | **Sector strip**   | Compact card grid, 4 per row (lg); smaller cards than §4; gap space-4.                                                                                          | 4 → 2 → 1; optional horizontal scroll on sm.                                                  | 12 sector cards from CPM §6. One link per sector with descriptive text.                                                                                                                                            |
| **7**  | **Featured proof** | Two-column: headline metric block + testimonial quote; padding space-8; tinted background.                                                                      | Columns stack; quote below metric on sm.                                                      | One case study + quote from CPM §7.1. Use &lt;blockquote&gt; + &lt;cite&gt;; portrait alt text; metric legible at AA contrast.                                                                                     |
| **8**  | **Resources**      | Three article cards in a row; card = image + tag + title + date.                                                                                                | 3 → 1 column; below md.                                                                       | Latest 3 resources (CPM §7.5). One link per card; publish date as &lt;time&gt;; image alt or decorative.                                                                                                           |
| **9**  | **CTA band**       | Full-width navy band; centred headline + two buttons; padding space-8.                                                                                          | Buttons stack full-width on sm (≥44px).                                                       | Final CTA block (CPM global). Primary "Request a Demo" fires cta_click → demo path (TSR §7.2). White text on navy meets AA; buttons keyboard-focusable.                                                            |
| **10** | **Footer**         | Mega-footer, 4-5 columns (modules, sectors, company, legal, social); padding space-8; hairline top border.                                                      | Columns collapse to stacked accordions on sm.                                                 | Footer navigation from SIA §3. &lt;footer&gt; landmark; link groups as &lt;ul&gt;; social icons have accessible names.                                                                                             |

**◫ ANNOTATED WIREFRAME - Module Page (template: module · applies to all 11)**

Read the numbered blocks top-to-bottom as the stacked page. Each number is annotated in the table beneath with its design spec, responsive behaviour, and content, interaction and accessibility notes.

**Annotation key**

| **#**  | **Element**        | **Design & spacing spec**                                                                                                             | **Responsive behaviour**                                                   | **Content · interaction · accessibility**                                                                                                                            |
| ------ | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **1**  | **Header**         | As homepage #1 - identical global component.                                                                                          | As homepage #1.                                                            | As homepage #1. Consistency across templates is mandatory.                                                                                                           |
| **2**  | **Breadcrumb**     | Small text row (§3.2 caption size); padding space-2; separator ›; sits directly under header.                                         | Truncates on sm, keeping the last two levels; horizontal scroll if needed. | Trail from IA (SIA breadcrumbs). Wrap in &lt;nav aria-label="Breadcrumb"&gt; and emit BreadcrumbList schema (TSR §6.3); current page is plain text, not a link.      |
| **3**  | **Hero (compact)** | Band, min-height ~360px; H1 = module name (§3.2), one-line outcome, module-tagged demo button, supporting visual; padding space-8.    | Two-column → stacked; visual below text on sm.                             | Overview/angle from CPM §5.3. Demo CTA carries the module parameter; page fires module_view (TSR §7.2). Single H1 = module name.                                     |
| **4**  | **Capabilities**   | Card grid ≥ 6 items, 3 per row (lg); icon + H3 + one line; gap space-4; equal height.                                                 | 3 → 2 → 1 column.                                                          | Capability list from CPM §5.3 capabilities table. Section led by an H2; each card titled with an H3; icons decorative.                                               |
| **5**  | **Outcomes**       | Benefit blocks on a tinted band; padding space-6; may alternate image/text rows.                                                      | Rows stack on sm.                                                          | Outcomes from CPM §5.3. Framed as results, not features; headings in logical order.                                                                                  |
| **6**  | **Screenshot**     | Product image (or placeholder slot) in a container max-width; radius + subtle shadow; lazy-loaded (TSR §4.3); optional call-out pins. | Scales to full container width; pins reposition or list below on sm.       | Screenshot from asset checklist (CPM §10). Descriptive alt or &lt;figure&gt;/&lt;figcaption&gt;; never convey key info by image alone.                               |
| **7**  | **Works with**     | 2-4 related-module cards; compact card variant; gap space-4.                                                                          | Row → stacked.                                                             | Related modules from CPM §5.3 "integrates with". Cross-sell links; one link per module with module-name text.                                                        |
| **8**  | **Proof**          | Testimonial or case-study card; tinted panel; padding space-6.                                                                        | Full-width stack.                                                          | Relevant quote/case (CPM §7.1). &lt;blockquote&gt; + &lt;cite&gt;; author portrait alt.                                                                              |
| **9**  | **FAQ**            | Accordion list; each row = question button + collapsible answer; divider tokens between rows.                                         | Full-width; comfortable 44px tap rows on sm.                               | Genuine Q&A (CPM); emit FAQPage schema (TSR §6.3). Each question is a &lt;button&gt; with aria-expanded / aria-controls; keyboard operable; not &lt;div&gt; toggles. |
| **10** | **CTA band**       | As homepage #9, but demo CTA is module-tagged.                                                                                        | As homepage #9.                                                            | Final CTA (CPM global). Fires cta_click with the module name; routes to demo path.                                                                                   |
| **11** | **Footer**         | As homepage #10.                                                                                                                      | As homepage #10.                                                           | As homepage #10.                                                                                                                                                     |

**◫ ANNOTATED WIREFRAME - Contact Page (template: contact) - form-focused**

Read the numbered blocks top-to-bottom as the stacked page. Each number is annotated in the table beneath with its design spec, responsive behaviour, and content, interaction and accessibility notes.

| **1** | **HERO "Talk to Marmidon" intro** |
| ----- | --------------------------------- |

| **2** | **CONTACT FORM Validated, routed enquiry form** |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------------- |

| **3** | **CHANNELS Phone, email, address, map, hours** |
| ----- | ---------------------------------------------- |

**Annotation key**

| **#** | **Element**        | **Design & spacing spec**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | **Responsive behaviour**                                                                                                  | **Content · interaction · accessibility**                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ----- | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **1** | **Hero (compact)** | Short band, min-height ~240px; H1 + one supporting line; padding space-6.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Text stays single column; scales down on sm.                                                                              | Intro from CPM §7.4. Single H1; no competing CTA above the form.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| **2** | **Contact form**   | Single-column form; label above each field; field height ~44px; space-4 between fields; radius token on inputs; error state uses coral (§2.2) plus text + icon, never colour alone; helper text under fields. Fields: Name, Company, Email, Sector (select), Message, consent checkbox, submit button (primary, §6.1). | Full-width single column at every breakpoint; inputs and submit are ≥44px touch targets; submit becomes full-width on sm. | Fields per CPM §7.4 / TSR §8. Inline + server-side validation (TSR §5.3); spam protection via CAPTCHA/honeypot; on success → thank-you page and contact_submit conversion (TSR §7.2); CRM + inbox delivery with fallback. Every field has a &lt;label for&gt;; errors are programmatic (aria-invalid, aria-describedby); required shown in text; consent required before submit; no keyboard trap; visible focus on all controls. |
| **3** | **Channels**       | Details block beside/under the form; icon + text rows; embedded map in a container with radius; padding space-6.                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Moves below the form and stacks on sm; map height reduces.                                                                | Contact details + map (CPM §7.4). Map is lazy-loaded and consent-aware (TSR §8.2). Address in &lt;address&gt;; phone/email are real links (tel:/mailto:); map has a text alternative (address) so it is not the only way to reach the info.                                                                                                                                                                                       |

**BEST PRACTICE · One annotation system, every template**

The three worked examples cover the site's highest-value and most interaction-heavy templates. Apply the same numbering-plus-annotation-key method to the remaining templates in §7.4-§7.5 during detailed design.

Because every annotation references the same tokens (§3), components (§6) and breakpoints (§5.2), annotated pages stay consistent with the rest of this document and with the build.

**SECTION 8**

**Accessibility in Design**

# **8\. Accessibility in Design (WCAG 2.2 AA)**

Accessibility is designed in, not bolted on. The following are design-side obligations; technical conformance detail is in TSR-004.

| **Area**   | **Design obligation**                                               |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Contrast   | All text/UI meets AA ratios; never colour-only meaning              |
| Focus      | Visible focus state on every interactive element                    |
| Targets    | Touch targets ≥ 44×44px; adequate spacing                           |
| Typography | Minimum 16px body; resizable to 200% without breakage               |
| Structure  | Logical heading order; landmarks; semantic components               |
| Forms      | Visible labels, accessible errors, instructions not by colour alone |
| Media      | Alt text for images; captions where relevant                        |
| Motion     | Honour prefers-reduced-motion; no flashing content                  |

**SECTION 9**

**Design Deliverables & Hand-off**

# **9\. Design Deliverables & Hand-off**

To realise this document, the design/build hand-off should include the following so Marmidon retains a maintainable, consistent system.

- A design-token sheet (colours, type scale, spacing, radius) implemented in code as variables.
- A component library / pattern set matching §6, with all states.
- High-fidelity designs for each template in §7 at mobile and desktop.
- The Marmidon logo as SVG (primary + reversed) plus favicon and social/OG image.
- Icon set covering the 11 modules and key UI actions.
- Image-handling spec (formats, sizes, alt-text policy) aligned to performance budget (TSR-004).

**NOTE · Consistency clause**

Because the same tokens and components render 11 module and 12 sector pages, any change to a token or component must be made centrally and cascade everywhere - never restyle a single page in isolation.

**- End of Document MARM-WEB-WDD-002 -**
